2606.32032.title: Reinforcement Learning with Metacognitive Feedback Elicits Faithful Uncertainty Expression in LLMs
2606.32032.paper_summary: Metacognition is a critical component of intelligence that describes the ability to monitor and regulate one's own cognitive processes. Yet LLMs exhibit systemic deficiencies in key metacognitive faculties: they hallucinate with high confidence, fail to recognize knowledge boundaries, and misrepresent their internal uncertainty--undermining trustworthiness and reliability. Since monitoring task performance and adapting behavior accordingly are central to metacognition, we posit that models capable of accurately judging their own performance are better positioned to improve it. We operationalize this idea via two novel mechanisms: reinforcement learning with metacognitive feedback (RLMF), a paradigm to refine completion rankings during preference optimization based on the quality of a model's self-judgments of performance, and metacognitive data selection, which uses similar self-judgments to identify high-value training examples, outperforming naive active learning. We apply these innovations to the problem of faithful calibration (FC), a task that is itself fundamentally metacognitive: the goal is to align expressed with intrinsic uncertainty, difficult even for frontier LLMs. We adopt a two-stage, decoupled approach, first using these methods to calibrate the faithfulness of models' self-reported confidence scores, then mapping to natural, context-adaptable linguistic uncertainty via targeted output editing. Extensive experiments show RLMF achieves generalizable, state-of-the-art FC on diverse tasks while preserving accuracy. Further, RLMF surpasses standard RL by up to 63% while enhancing models' ability to assess and express their own capability limits. This positions RLMF as a promising paradigm to enhance LLM metacognition toward improved abilities and alignment, and suggests metacognitive performance as an effective RL signal to overcome limits of prior intrinsic feedback methods.
2606.32032.paper_summary_zh: 元認知是智力的一個關鍵組成部分，描述了監控和調節自己認知過程的能力。<lb/>然而，LLMs在關鍵的元認知能力上表現出系統性的缺陷：它們以高度自信的方式出現幻覺，未能識別知識邊界，並錯誤地表現其內部不確定性——這削弱了可信度和可靠性。<lb/>由於監控任務表現並根據需要調整行為是元認知的核心，我們假設能夠準確評估自己表現的模型更能改善其表現。<lb/>我們通過兩種新穎的機制來具體化這一想法：帶有元認知反饋的強化學習（RLMF），一種在基於模型自我評估表現質量的偏好優化過程中細化完成排名的範式，以及元認知數據選擇，利用類似的自我評估來識別高價值的訓練範例，超越天真的主動學習。<lb/>我們將這些創新應用於忠實校準（FC）問題，這是一項本質上也是元認知的任務：其目標是使表達的不確定性與內在不確定性對齊，這對於前沿的LLMs來說甚至是困難的。<lb/>我們採用兩階段的解耦方法，首先使用這些方法來校準模型自我報告的信心分數的忠實度，然後通過目標輸出編輯映射到自然的、可適應上下文的語言不確定性。<lb/>大量實驗顯示，RLMF在多樣任務上實現了可泛化的、最先進的FC，同時保持了準確性。<lb/>此外，RLMF在提升模型評估和表達自身能力限制的能力方面，超越了標準的強化學習，達到63%。<lb/>這使得RLMF成為一種有前景的範式，旨在增強LLM的元認知，以改善能力和對齊，並且建議將元認知表現作為一種有效的強化學習信號，以克服先前內在反饋方法的限制。
2606.32032.author: Gabrielle Kaili-May Liu et.al.
2606.32032.authors: Gabrielle Kaili-May Liu, Avi Caciularu, Gal Yona, Idan Szpektor, Arman Cohan
